interactions.mechanism: Vitamin K antagonises warfarin's anticoagulant effect by replenishing the clotting factor cofactor warfarin blocks.
interactions.clinical_effect: Reduced INR, increased clotting risk if intake fluctuates.
interactions.recommendation: Maintain consistent daily vitamin K intake rather than avoiding leafy greens entirely.
interactions.evidence_source: FDA label; Holbrook et al., Arch Intern Med 2005

substitutions.rationale: Far lower vitamin K (~10 ug/cup vs 500 ug/cup)
Substantially lower vitamin K per cooked cup

interactions.mechanism: Cranberry flavonoids inhibit CYP2C9, the principal enzyme that metabolises S-warfarin, raising plasma warfarin concentrations.
interactions.clinical_effect: Elevated INR with bleeding risk reported in case series with high cranberry juice intake.
interactions.recommendation: Limit cranberry juice to occasional small servings; monitor INR closely if regular intake is desired.
interactions.evidence_source: FDA warfarin label; Suvarna et al., BMJ 2003 (case report)

substitutions.rationale: Comparable tartness without documented CYP2C9 inhibition at typical intake

interactions.mechanism: MAO inhibitors block the deamination of dietary tyramine, allowing systemic tyramine to displace norepinephrine and trigger a hypertensive crisis.
interactions.clinical_effect: Severe headache, hypertensive crisis, intracranial hemorrhage, possible death.
interactions.recommendation: Strictly avoid tyramine-rich foods during therapy and for 2 weeks after discontinuation.
interactions.evidence_source: FDA Nardil label; Gillman, Br J Anaesth 2005

substitutions.rationale: Not fermented from soy proteins; very low tyramine

interactions.mechanism: Same MAO-mediated tyramine accumulation; tranylcypromine is a particularly potent and irreversible MAO-A/B inhibitor.
interactions.clinical_effect: Hypertensive crisis with stroke risk.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid all aged, fermented, smoked, pickled, or spoiled protein-containing foods. Provide patient with full dietary list.
interactions.evidence_source: FDA Parnate label

interactions.mechanism: Furanocoumarins in grapefruit irreversibly inhibit intestinal CYP3A4, increasing oral bioavailability of dihydropyridine CCBs.
interactions.clinical_effect: Excessive vasodilation, hypotension, peripheral edema, reflex tachycardia.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid grapefruit and grapefruit juice during amlodipine therapy.
interactions.evidence_source: Bailey et al., CMAJ 2013; FDA Norvasc label

substitutions.rationale: Sweet oranges do not contain the relevant furanocoumarins

interactions.mechanism: Grapefruit furanocoumarins inhibit intestinal CYP3A4, dramatically raising simvastatin AUC (up to 3.6-fold).
interactions.clinical_effect: Markedly elevated risk of myopathy and rhabdomyolysis.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid grapefruit completely while on simvastatin; consider switching to pravastatin or rosuvastatin if grapefruit intake is essential.
interactions.evidence_source: FDA Zocor label; Lilja et al., Clin Pharmacol Ther 2004

substitutions.rationale: Comparable vitamin C without CYP3A4 inhibition

interactions.mechanism: CYP3A4 inhibition raises atorvastatin exposure, though to a lesser degree than simvastatin.
interactions.clinical_effect: Increased risk of myalgia and transaminase elevation.
interactions.recommendation: Limit grapefruit to small, infrequent servings; avoid altogether at higher atorvastatin doses (>=40 mg/day).
interactions.evidence_source: FDA Lipitor label

interactions.mechanism: Divalent cations (Ca2+, Mg2+, Fe2+) chelate tetracyclines in the gut, forming insoluble complexes that are not absorbed.
interactions.clinical_effect: Dramatically reduced antibiotic plasma levels; treatment failure.
interactions.recommendation: Take tetracycline at least 2 hours before or 4 hours after dairy products, calcium supplements, or antacids.
interactions.evidence_source: FDA tetracycline label; Neuvonen, Drugs 1976

substitutions.with: water with medication, dairy 2+ hours later

interactions.mechanism: Divalent cation chelation reduces doxycycline absorption, though less severely than tetracycline.
interactions.clinical_effect: Reduced antibiotic exposure; possible treatment failure.
interactions.recommendation: Separate doxycycline from dairy and calcium supplements by at least 2 hours.
interactions.evidence_source: FDA Vibramycin label

substitutions.rationale: Avoids chelation while still allowing dairy at other times

interactions.mechanism: Fluoroquinolones chelate divalent and trivalent cations in the GI tract, blocking absorption.
interactions.clinical_effect: Subtherapeutic antibiotic levels and treatment failure.
interactions.recommendation: Take ciprofloxacin 2 hours before or 6 hours after dairy, antacids, iron, or multivitamins with minerals.
interactions.evidence_source: FDA Cipro label; Neuvonen et al., Antimicrob Agents Chemother 1991

substitutions.rationale: Avoids cation chelation; yogurt may be consumed >6 hours later

interactions.mechanism: Same divalent/trivalent cation chelation that affects ciprofloxacin.
interactions.clinical_effect: Reduced absorption and possible therapeutic failure.
interactions.recommendation: Take levofloxacin 2 hours before or 2 hours after antacids, sucralfate, dairy, or mineral supplements.
interactions.evidence_source: FDA Levaquin label

interactions.mechanism: Soy isoflavones bind levothyroxine in the gut and inhibit its absorption.
interactions.clinical_effect: Subtherapeutic thyroid hormone levels, persistent hypothyroid symptoms, elevated TSH.
interactions.recommendation: Take levothyroxine on an empty stomach and separate from soy-containing foods or supplements by at least 4 hours.
interactions.evidence_source: Bell & Ovalle, Endocr Pract 2001; FDA Synthroid label

substitutions.with: lactose-free dairy milk or oat milk
substitutions.rationale: No isoflavones; consume >=4 hours from dose

interactions.mechanism: Calcium binds levothyroxine in the gut, forming a non-absorbable complex.
interactions.clinical_effect: Reduced T4 absorption and rising TSH.
interactions.recommendation: Separate calcium-containing foods or supplements from levothyroxine by at least 4 hours.
interactions.evidence_source: Singh et al., JAMA 2000

interactions.mechanism: Soluble fiber adsorbs levothyroxine in the gut, decreasing systemic absorption.
interactions.clinical_effect: Erratic thyroid control if fiber intake is inconsistent.
interactions.recommendation: Take levothyroxine on an empty stomach with water; allow 4 hours before high-fiber meals or psyllium.
interactions.evidence_source: Liel et al., Am J Med 1996

interactions.mechanism: ACE inhibitors reduce aldosterone-driven potassium excretion; concurrent high potassium intake or potassium-based salt substitutes can drive serum K+ above 5.5 mEq/L.
interactions.clinical_effect: Hyperkalemia with risk of cardiac arrhythmia.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid potassium-containing salt substitutes; do not start potassium supplements without monitoring; review high-potassium food intake especially in CKD.
interactions.evidence_source: FDA lisinopril label; Palmer, NEJM 2004

interactions.mechanism: ACE inhibition decreases aldosterone, raising serum potassium.
interactions.clinical_effect: Hyperkalemia risk, especially with concurrent potassium-sparing diuretics or CKD.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid potassium salt substitutes; review supplements; routinely monitor serum potassium.
interactions.evidence_source: FDA Vasotec label

interactions.mechanism: Alcohol potentiates metformin's effect on lactate metabolism, raising the risk of lactic acidosis, and can independently cause hypoglycemia.
interactions.clinical_effect: Lactic acidosis (rare but life-threatening); hypoglycemia.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid acute heavy alcohol intake; limit chronic intake to <=1 drink/day for women, <=2 for men, with meals.
interactions.evidence_source: FDA Glucophage label

interactions.mechanism: Linezolid is a reversible MAO inhibitor; dietary tyramine cannot be deaminated and triggers a hypertensive reaction.
interactions.clinical_effect: Hypertensive crisis.
interactions.recommendation: Limit dietary tyramine to <100 mg per meal during therapy; avoid aged, fermented, pickled, or spoiled foods.
interactions.evidence_source: FDA Zyvox label

interactions.mechanism: Soluble and insoluble fibers bind digoxin in the gut, decreasing oral bioavailability.
interactions.clinical_effect: Subtherapeutic digoxin levels and loss of rate control or contractility benefit.
interactions.recommendation: Separate digoxin doses from high-fiber meals or psyllium by at least 2 hours.
interactions.evidence_source: Brown et al., J Clin Pharmacol 1985

interactions.mechanism: Calcium and other polyvalent cations chelate bisphosphonates and prevent absorption (oral bioavailability is already <1%).
interactions.clinical_effect: Loss of antiresorptive effect; treatment failure.
interactions.recommendation: Take alendronate first thing in morning with plain water only; wait at least 30 minutes before any food, beverage, or other medication.
interactions.evidence_source: FDA Fosamax label

substitutions.with: plain water with medication; coffee 30+ min later

interactions.mechanism: Coffee and orange juice independently reduce alendronate absorption by ~60% even without polyvalent cations.
interactions.clinical_effect: Reduced antiresorptive efficacy.
interactions.recommendation: Take with plain tap water only; wait at least 30 minutes before coffee, tea, or juice.
interactions.evidence_source: Gertz et al., Clin Pharmacol Ther 1995

interactions.mechanism: Polyphenols (tannins) in tea bind non-heme iron, forming insoluble complexes in the gut.
interactions.clinical_effect: Reduced iron absorption; slower correction of iron deficiency anemia.
interactions.recommendation: Take iron with vitamin C (e.g., orange juice); avoid tea within 1-2 hours of dosing.
interactions.evidence_source: Hurrell et al., Br J Nutr 1999

interactions.mechanism: Coffee polyphenols (chlorogenic acid) inhibit non-heme iron absorption.
interactions.clinical_effect: Suboptimal repletion in iron-deficiency anemia.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid coffee within 1 hour before or after iron dose.
interactions.evidence_source: Morck et al., Am J Clin Nutr 1983

interactions.mechanism: Calcium competes with iron for the divalent metal transporter (DMT1) in the duodenum.
interactions.clinical_effect: Reduced iron absorption when taken concurrently with dairy or calcium supplements.
interactions.recommendation: Separate iron and calcium-containing foods or supplements by at least 2 hours.
interactions.evidence_source: Hallberg et al., Am J Clin Nutr 1991

interactions.mechanism: Lithium and sodium share renal proximal tubule reabsorption. Low sodium intake or sodium loss increases lithium reabsorption; high sodium increases lithium excretion.
interactions.clinical_effect: Sudden sodium drops can cause lithium toxicity (tremor, confusion, seizures); sudden sodium loads can drop lithium below therapeutic range.
interactions.recommendation: Maintain consistent dietary sodium; avoid sudden low-sodium diets, fasting, or heavy sweating without fluid/electrolyte replacement.
interactions.evidence_source: FDA Lithobid label; Finley, Clin Pharmacokinet 2016

interactions.mechanism: Both SSRIs and tryptophan/5-HTP raise serotonergic tone; combined use can produce serotonin syndrome.
interactions.clinical_effect: Agitation, hyperreflexia, clonus, hyperthermia, autonomic instability.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid tryptophan or 5-HTP supplements while on SSRIs; counsel patients about herbal serotonergic agents (St. John's wort).
interactions.evidence_source: Boyer & Shannon, NEJM 2005

substitutions.with: discontinue and rely on prescribed SSRI

interactions.mechanism: Additive serotonergic activity raises risk of serotonin syndrome.
interactions.clinical_effect: Serotonin syndrome.
interactions.recommendation: Do not combine SSRIs with tryptophan or 5-HTP supplements.
interactions.evidence_source: FDA Prozac label

interactions.mechanism: Red yeast rice contains monacolin K, which is biochemically identical to lovastatin; combined with a prescription statin it produces additive HMG-CoA reductase inhibition.
interactions.clinical_effect: Doubled exposure raises risk of myopathy, rhabdomyolysis, and hepatotoxicity.
interactions.recommendation: Do not use red yeast rice supplements while on any prescription statin.
interactions.evidence_source: Cohen et al., JAMA Intern Med 2017

substitutions.rationale: Prescription statin already provides full therapeutic effect

interactions.mechanism: Monacolin K stacks with prescription statin activity, producing additive HMG-CoA reductase inhibition.
interactions.clinical_effect: Increased risk of myopathy and rhabdomyolysis.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid red yeast rice while on any prescription statin.
interactions.evidence_source: Cohen et al., JAMA Intern Med 2017

substitutions.rationale: Statin already maximally inhibits the same enzyme

interactions.mechanism: Spironolactone blocks aldosterone-mediated potassium excretion; high dietary potassium or potassium salts can cause severe hyperkalemia.
interactions.clinical_effect: Hyperkalemia and cardiac arrhythmia.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid potassium-based salt substitutes; do not start potassium supplements without monitoring.
interactions.evidence_source: FDA Aldactone label

interactions.mechanism: Grapefruit furanocoumarins inhibit intestinal CYP3A4, the principal enzyme metabolising diazepam. This increases oral bioavailability and prolongs sedative effects.
interactions.clinical_effect: Excessive sedation, prolonged psychomotor impairment, increased risk of falls and respiratory depression. Clinically meaningful interaction documented at typical grapefruit serving sizes.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid grapefruit and grapefruit juice while on diazepam. The interaction persists for 24-72 hours after grapefruit consumption due to irreversible CYP3A4 inhibition.
interactions.evidence_source: Bailey et al., CMAJ 2013; Ozdemir et al., J Clin Pharmacol 1998

substitutions.rationale: Sweet oranges lack the relevant furanocoumarins

interactions.mechanism: Both diazepam and alcohol are CNS depressants acting on GABA-A receptors. Combined use produces synergistic central nervous system and respiratory depression. Alcohol additionally lowers seizure threshold, particularly relevant in patients with underlying epilepsy.
interactions.clinical_effect: Profound sedation, respiratory depression, impaired psychomotor function, potential for fatal overdose. In epilepsy patients, paradoxical seizure risk during alcohol withdrawal.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid all alcoholic beverages while on diazepam. This is especially critical in patients with seizure disorders where alcohol independently lowers seizure threshold.
interactions.evidence_source: FDA Valium label; Tanaka, J Clin Pharm Ther 2003

substitutions.with: sparkling water with citrus or non-alcoholic wine

substitutions.rationale: Maintains social drinking ritual without ethanol

interactions.mechanism: Citrus and apple juices inhibit organic anion transporting polypeptides (OATP1A2 and OATP2B1) in the small intestine. These transporters mediate fexofenadine uptake, so juice co-administration can reduce fexofenadine bioavailability by up to 70%.
interactions.clinical_effect: Reduced antihistamine efficacy with breakthrough allergy symptoms; the effect is most pronounced when juice is taken simultaneously with the dose.
interactions.recommendation: Take fexofenadine with plain water. Avoid fruit juices for at least 4 hours before and 1-2 hours after dosing.
interactions.evidence_source: Dresser et al., Clin Pharmacol Ther 2002; Bailey, Br J Clin Pharmacol 2010

substitutions.with: water with morning medication; juice 2+ hours later

interactions.mechanism: Caffeine is a CNS stimulant that pharmacodynamically antagonises the sedative effects of benzodiazepines. The interaction does not alter diazepam pharmacokinetics but reduces perceived efficacy.
interactions.clinical_effect: Reduced anxiolytic and sedative effect of diazepam; potentially counterproductive in anxiety or seizure prophylaxis contexts.
interactions.recommendation: Moderate caffeine intake, especially around dosing times. Be aware that high caffeine intake may mask appropriate sedation needed for clinical effect.
interactions.evidence_source: Mattila et al., Eur J Clin Pharmacol 1992
